questions.q: What is the mass of a nucleon?
questions.answer: The mass of a nucleon is approximately 1.67 × 10^-27 kg.

questions.q: State the law of conservation of mass in nuclear reactions.
questions.answer: The total mass of reactants in a nuclear reaction is equal to the total mass of products.

questions.q: What is the difference between a stable nucleus and an unstable nucleus?
questions.answer: A stable nucleus is one that does not undergo radioactive decay, whereas an unstable nucleus undergoes radioactive decay to become stable.

questions.q: What is the effect of binding energy on the stability of a nucleus?
questions.answer: A higher binding energy per nucleon indicates a more stable nucleus.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the Geiger counter?
questions.answer: A Geiger counter is used to measure the radiation level of a substance.

questions.q: Describe the process of alpha decay.
questions.answer: In alpha decay, an unstable nucleus emits an alpha particle (two protons and two neutrons) to become more stable.

questions.q: Explain the process of beta decay.
questions.answer: In beta decay, an unstable nucleus emits either a beta particle (an electron) or a positron to become more stable.

questions.q: What is the relation between binding energy and mass defect?
questions.answer: The mass defect is equal to the binding energy of the nucleus divided by c^2, where c is the speed of light.

questions.q: Discuss the significance of half-life in radioactive decay.
questions.answer: The half-life of a radioactive substance is a constant value that represents the time required for half of the initial amount of the substance to decay.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a radiation detector?
questions.answer: A radiation detector is used to measure the level of radiation in a substance or environment.

questions.q: Describe the process of nuclear fission.
questions.answer: In nuclear fission, an unstable nucleus splits into two or more smaller nuclei, releasing a large amount of energy in the process.

questions.q: Explain the process of nuclear fusion.
questions.answer: In nuclear fusion, two or more small nuclei combine to form a single larger nucleus, releasing a large amount of energy in the process.

questions.q: Discuss the concept of nuclear binding energy per nucleon.
questions.answer: The binding energy per nucleon is a measure of the stability of a nucleus, with higher values indicating more stable nuclei.

questions.q: Explain the working principle of a Geiger counter.
questions.answer: A Geiger counter works by detecting the ionization caused by radiation and converting it into an electrical signal that can be measured.

questions.q: Describe the process of alpha, beta, and gamma radiation.
questions.answer: Alpha radiation is the emission of alpha particles, beta radiation is the emission of beta particles, and gamma radiation is the emission of gamma rays, all of which occur during radioactive decay.
